phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to Old Morrison, the main building
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, pouvez-vous m'indiquer le bâtiment Old Morrison ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-KYOO-zay mwah, poo-VAY voo man-dee-KAY luh bat-ee-MAHN old MOR-ee-sun ?
phrases.cultural_note: Old Morrison was built in the Greek Revival style popular in the early American republic — a style heavily influenced by French and European classical architecture ideals.

responses.french: Vous ne pouvez pas le manquer — c'est le grand bâtiment à colonnes blanches au centre du campus.
responses.english: You can't miss it — it's the large building with white columns in the center of campus.
responses.phonetic: voo nuh poo-VAY pah luh mahn-KAY — say luh grahn bat-ee-MAHN ah koh-LOHN BLANSH oh sahn-truh doo kahm-POOS.

phrases.text_local: Ce campus est vraiment superbe. L'architecture est magnifique.
phrases.pronunciation: suh kahm-POOS ay vray-MAHN soo-PAIRB. lar-shee-tek-TOOR ay mah-nyee-FEEK.
phrases.cultural_note: Americans often overlook the fact that many early American universities were deliberately designed in styles inspired by European — including French — classical traditions.

phrases.intent_en: Mention you are just visiting the campus
phrases.text_local: Je suis juste en visite. C'est la première fois que je vois ce campus.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh swee zhoos ahn vee-ZEET. say lah pruh-MYAIR fwah kuh zhuh VWAH suh kahm-POOS.
phrases.cultural_note: Campus visits are a relaxed, open affair at American universities — very different from many French grandes écoles, which have more restricted access. Mentioning you're a first-time visitor is always a friendly opener.

phrases.text_local: Quelle est l'histoire de cette université ? Elle semble très ancienne.
phrases.pronunciation: kel ay lees-TWAHR duh set oo-nee-vair-see-TAY ? el sah-MBLUH tray ahn-SYEN.
phrases.cultural_note: Asking about history ('l'histoire') is a deeply French instinct — France has a strong culture of historical consciousness. Showing this curiosity will be warmly received on any old campus.

responses.french: C'est l'une des plus vieilles universités des États-Unis, fondée en 1780.
responses.english: It's one of the oldest universities in the United States, founded in 1780.
responses.phonetic: say loon day ploo VYAY oo-nee-vair-see-TAY day zay-tah-ZOO-nee, fohn-DAY ahn mil-SET-sahn-kah-TRE.

phrases.intent_en: Comment that it's the oldest college west of the Alleghenies
phrases.text_local: Je viens de lire que c'est le plus vieux collège à l'ouest des Appalaches. C'est impressionnant !
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh VYAN duh LEER kuh say luh ploo VYUH koh-LEZH ah loo-AY day zah-pah-LASH. say tan-press-yoh-NAHN !
phrases.cultural_note: Founded in 1780, Transylvania predates most of American civilization west of the East Coast. Framing this as a discovery ('je viens de lire' — I just read) makes for natural, enthusiastic conversation.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there is a campus café or bookshop
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a un café ou une librairie sur le campus ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-keel ee ah un kah-FAY oo oon lee-brai-REE soor luh kahm-POOS ?
phrases.cultural_note: 'Une librairie' in French is a bookshop — not a library (which is 'une bibliothèque'). This is one of the most common and entertaining false friends for English speakers learning French.

responses.french: Oui, il y a un café et une librairie universitaire dans le bâtiment d'accueil.
responses.english: Yes, there's a café and a university bookshop in the welcome building.
responses.phonetic: WEE, eel ee ah un kah-FAY ay oon lee-brai-REE oo-nee-vair-see-TAIR dahn luh bat-ee-MAHN dah-KWHY.
